rulings.Case Title: ZF MICRO SOLUTIONS INC. et al VS. GORDON & REES LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Compliance With The Court'S Prior Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, February 24, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT ILLINOIS UNION INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Compliance With The Court's Prior Order. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed; motion granted. Plaintiffs shall fully comply with the Court order dated 12/20/13 no later than ten days after notice of entry of this order. Sanctions of $600 are awarded against Plaintiffs in addition to the $560 previously ordered. All sanctions shall be paid within ten days after notice of entry of this order. Any further violation of this Court's orders may result in imposition of issue preclusion or terminating sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: UNIFIED WEBHOSTING, LLC VS. DIGITAL HEMISPHERE DEVELOPMENT, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 24, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANT COMMERCEGATE IRELAND LIMITED's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS Denied. (CCP § 416.10.) Plaintiff has demonstrated that minimum contacts exist between Defendant Commerce Gate Ireland Limited ("Defendant") and the State of California. (CCP § 410.10.) On its website, Defendant identified an office located at 501 Santa Monica Blvd., Ste. 506, Santa Monica, CA 90401. (Yaport Decl., Exhibit A.) Further, by its own admission, Defendant permitted other parties, including Defendant Digital Hemisphere Development, LLC, to communicate with third parties under its domain for marketing and branding recognition. (Skarlen Reply Decl., 8.) This includes communications sent to Plaintiff from Andy Chong and Jen Miller regarding the subject hosting facility. (Schwing Decl., 3 - 5, Exhibits A, B.) However, Plaintiff has not demonstrated effective service. (CCP § 416.10.) On the proof of service, the process server served by substitute service on "Gary Cann, Agent for Service of Process." (Proof of Service, filed Sept. 18, 2013, 3b.) However, Defendant has not appointed an agent for service of process. Further, Plaintiff has not demonstrated that Andy Chong is a "president, chief executive officer, or other head of the corporation, a vice president, a secretary or assistant secretary, a treasurer or assistant treasurer, a controller or chief financial officer, a general manager, or a person authorized by the corporation to receive service of process." (CCP § 416.10(b).) In email correspondence to Plaintiff, Mr. Chong identified himself as an Accountant for CommerceGate. (Schwing Decl., Exhibit A.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/ehg

rulings.Case Title: ALAN MOSS VS. KENDRA R. GILES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 24, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANT THE HERTZ CORPORATION 's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend. The Court takes judicial notice of the complaint and judgment in the small claims action (CSM-12-840669) pursuant to Evidence Code sec. 452(d). Both the small claims complaint and the complaint in this action assert claims for negligence, infliction of emotional distress, personal injury, and property damages arising out of the same car accident. Res judicata bars Plaintiff's claims in this action. (See Sanderson v. Niemann (1941) 17 Cal.2d 563, 573, "a judgment of any court whether of high or low jurisdiction, and of record or not, constitutes a complete bar against a second suit on the same cause of action.") A general demurrer lies where the facts alleged in the complaint or matters judicially noticed show that plaintiff is seeking relief from the same defendant on the same cause of action. (Boeken v. Philip Morris (2010) 48 Cal.4th 788.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/ehg

rulings.Case Title: FOODCARE INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION (FORMERLY VS. FOODCALC LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 24, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANTS FOODCALC LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY, GEOFFREY NEEDHAM, MAUREEN NEEDHAM AND NEEDHAM, LUCY (SUED AS LUCY NEEDHAM A.K.A. LUCY DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained, without leave to amend. (CCP § 430.10(e).) Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege breach of an implied contract. "If facts appearing in the exhibits contradict those alleged, the facts in the exhibits take precedence." (Holland v. Morse Diesel Intern., Inc. (2001) 86 Cal.App.4th 1443, 1447.) The facts in the exhibits demonstrate that there was no mutual assent between the parties. (See SAC, Exhibits A - C.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
